patch.value: Docstring coverage regressed below achieved-100% by PR#536 extraction files; quality:static floors never ratcheted and mask drift; drop dead closure-pattern export

runtime-utils.ts and sdk.ts in packages/pm-governance-audit/extensions/governance-audit carry module + exported + member docstrings and static-quality-gate reports 100/100/100 with default thresholds; DEFAULT_CLOSURE_LIKE_METADATA_FIELD_PATTERNS is removed from runtime-utils.ts (zero call sites) or wired to a real consumer with a covering spec; the quality:static script floors in package.json are ratcheted to the newly achieved coverage so future sub-100% files fail CI

## Evidence (review pass 2026-07-13, gate-verified)

Running `node scripts/release/static-quality-gate.mjs` with DEFAULT thresholds (100%) fails on exactly two files, both introduced by PR#536 (governance-audit extraction phase 2):

- `packages/pm-governance-audit/extensions/governance-audit/runtime-utils.ts` — missing module docstring, 13 exported docstrings, 5 member docstrings (LinkedArtifactAuditPayload/LinkedArtifactAuditEntry fields).
- `packages/pm-governance-audit/extensions/governance-audit/sdk.ts` — missing module docstring + 2 exported docstrings (line 26 exported value, RuntimeStatusRegistry line 50).

Achieved coverage today: source 99.29%, exported 99.41%, member 99.89%. The repo previously reached literal 100% via the closed docstring waves (pm-4ak1 repo-wide, pm-qely packages/*). CI stays green because `quality:static` in package.json passes floors `--min-docstring-coverage 92.93 --min-exported-docstring-coverage 84.41 --min-member-docstring-coverage 13.655` — set before the waves completed and never ratcheted, so ~6-15 points of headroom silently absorb regressions. Ratcheting the floors is the control that makes the next extraction-style PR fail instead of drift.

## Dead export (same files, same fix window)

`DEFAULT_CLOSURE_LIKE_METADATA_FIELD_PATTERNS` (runtime-utils.ts:3) has ZERO call sites across src/, tests/, packages/, scripts/ — including inside runtime-utils.ts itself. normalize.ts derives its closure-like patterns from `settings.validation.lifecycle_closure_like_*_patterns` (normalize.ts:202-208), so the constant is an extraction leftover default table nobody reads. Contrast: `buildLinkedArtifactAudit` LOOKS test-only from an external grep but is called internally at runtime-utils.ts:166 and powers the shipped `pm files/docs --audit` flag (verified live) — do NOT remove it.

Not covered by: pm-u5zr (windows spec exemption), pm-rxp1 CLOSED (boundary spec), pm-j8z6 (file links), pm-92if (CodeFactor-scoped).
quality:static floors 92.93/84.41/13.655 need ratchet to achieved coverage
